summary: The primary lymphocyte types present in the splenic pulp proper include both B-cells from the germinal centers and T-cells distributed throughout other areas such as the marginal zones; these findings support option C being correct for identifying predominant cell populations therein. Agent1 reasoned by focusing on where different classes of lymphocytes reside based on anatomical descriptions provided about the structure of the spleen’s white pulp area, emphasizing their roles related directly back towards answering which type would predominantly be located "in the pulp proper". This led them specifically toward selecting an answer involving a mix between B-cell presence due its central role versus broader representation including T-cells across various sub-regions noted
